tasks.title: Project Initialization and Environment Setup
tasks.description: Establish the Python-based project structure, dependency management, and base configuration system.
tasks.details: 1. Create the directory structure: `src/data`, `src/models`, `src/engine`, `src/api`, `configs/`, and `logs/`. 2. Initialize `pyproject.toml` or `requirements.txt` with foundational libraries: `torch` or `tensorflow`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scikit-learn`, `fastapi`, and `pydantic`. 3. Implement a YAML-based configuration loader to manage hyperparameters, data paths, and system settings.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that all directories exist and that the environment can be recreated using the dependency file. Validate that the configuration loader correctly parses sample YAML files.

tasks.title: Data Ingestion and Preprocessing Pipeline
tasks.description: Develop a module to load, clean, transform, and split datasets into training and validation sets.
tasks.details: 1. Implement a `DataLoader` class capable of reading CSV/Parquet files. 2. Include methods for handling missing values (imputation/removal) and categorical encoding. 3. Integrate feature scaling (MinMax or Standard) using Scikit-Learn. 4. Implement a reproducible split function using a random seed.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test each preprocessing step (e.g., verify no NaNs remain after cleaning). Assert that the split ratio is correctly applied and that training/validation sets are disjoint.

tasks.title: Configurable Neural Network Architecture
tasks.description: Implement a modular neural network class that supports configurable layers and activation functions.
tasks.details: 1. Create a `NeuralNet` class inheriting from `torch.nn.Module`. 2. Use the configuration file to dynamically build layers (Linear/Dense). 3. Support various activation functions (ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh) passed via parameters. 4. Ensure the output layer matches the prediction task (Regression/Classification).
tasks.testStrategy: Instantiate models with different configurations (e.g., 3 layers vs 5 layers) and verify the parameter count and layer types match the definition.

tasks.title: Loss and Optimization Module
tasks.description: Configure the loss functions and optimization strategies for the training process.
tasks.details: 1. Create a factory for loss functions (MSELoss, CrossEntropyLoss) and optimizers (Adam, SGD). 2. Implement a learning rate scheduler strategy (e.g., StepLR or ReduceLROnPlateau). 3. Ensure these are linked to the global configuration file for easy experimentation.
tasks.testStrategy: Check if gradients are being calculated correctly after a dummy forward pass and ensure the optimizer updates weights.

tasks.title: Training Engine Implementation
tasks.description: Build the core training loop including epoch control, batch processing, and backpropagation.
tasks.details: 1. Implement a `Trainer` class that manages the loop. 2. Integrate the `DataLoader` for batching. 3. Implement forward pass, loss calculation, backward pass, and parameter updates. 4. Add logic for tracking loss per epoch and printing progress.
tasks.testStrategy: Run training on a small synthetic dataset and verify that the loss decreases over time. Ensure the loop stops correctly after the specified number of epochs.

tasks.title: Evaluation and Metrics Suite
tasks.description: Develop a suite to calculate performance metrics such as Accuracy, F1 Score, and Loss.
tasks.details: 1. Create an `Evaluator` class that puts the model in evaluation mode (`model.eval()`). 2. Implement logic to calculate Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1 Score using `sklearn.metrics`. 3. Output a detailed evaluation report after each validation run or at the end of training.
tasks.testStrategy: Compare the Evaluator's output against manually calculated metrics for a fixed set of predictions and ground truth labels.

tasks.title: Model Serialization and Checkpointing
tasks.description: Implement logic to save and restore model weights, optimizer states, and training metadata.
tasks.details: 1. Add a checkpointing mechanism to the `Trainer` to save the best model based on validation loss. 2. Implement `save_model` and `load_model` functions using `torch.save` or equivalent. 3. Include training state metadata (epoch number, seed) in the checkpoint file.
tasks.testStrategy: Train a model for 2 epochs, save it, load it back, and ensure that continuing training from epoch 3 yields consistent results.

tasks.title: Inference Engine and Wrapper
tasks.description: Develop a prediction wrapper that converts raw input features into model-ready tensors and returns results.
tasks.details: 1. Implement an `InferenceEngine` class that loads a serialized model. 2. Reuse the preprocessing logic from Task 22 to transform new, unseen input data. 3. Handle tensor conversion and batching for inference requests.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that providing a single raw input record results in a prediction that matches the expected shape and data type.

tasks.title: Prediction API Development
tasks.description: Create a FastAPI application to serve model predictions through a REST endpoint.
tasks.details: 1. Define a Pydantic model for input data validation. 2. Create a POST endpoint `/predict` that triggers the `InferenceEngine`. 3. Add a GET endpoint `/health` to check system status. 4. Implement basic error handling for invalid input data.
tasks.testStrategy: Start the API server and send a POST request with valid JSON data; verify the response contains a prediction. Send invalid data and verify a 422 error is returned.

tasks.title: Logging, Monitoring, and Deployment Setup
tasks.description: Add system-wide logging and prepare the environment for deployment using Docker.
tasks.details: 1. Configure the Python `logging` module to output to both console and `logs/` directory. 2. Log system events, training progress, and API request metadata. 3. Create a `Dockerfile` for the API and training worker. 4. Provide a `README.md` with instructions for setup and deployment.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that logs are correctly populated in the log file. Build the Docker image and ensure the container starts without errors.
